title: Extending qryflow Functionality
text: qryflow designed easily extended, allowing users define custom chunk types. vignette provides relevant background knowledge qryflow works hood, walks create register custom chunk types. vignette assumes knowledge found “Getting Started” (vignette("getting-started", package = "qryflow")) vignette.

title: Extending qryflow Functionality
text: run SQL script using qryflow, process follows steps: Split SQL script chunks using tagged comments (e.g., -- @query: name) Parse chunk, capturing type, name, tags Execute chunk using type-specific handler support new chunk type, ’ll need : Create handler — defines execute chunk return results. Register new type qryflow package knows process .

title: Extending qryflow Functionality
text: chunk type needs associated handler. Handlers accept qryflow_chunk object database connection object (e.g., DBI::dbConnect). execute SQL appropriate return result: handler “exec” type: custom handler created, needs validated registered.
code: qryflow_exec_handler <- function(con, chunk, ...) {      # Pass the SQL of the chunk to desired execution strategy   result <- DBI::dbExecute(con, chunk$sql, ...)    # Return the result   result }

title: Extending qryflow Functionality
text: qryflow provides validate_qryflow_handler() test whether handler function meets specifications. error occur : object function formal arguments included formal arguments right order Note: test code within function correct verify correct output type.
code: validate_qryflow_handler(qryflow_exec_handler)

title: Extending qryflow Functionality
text: qryflow maintains internal environment called .qryflow_handlers store registered chunk handlers. package loaded, default types like “query” “exec” automatically registered. can register additional types using: validate handler registering internal environment. can access types registered: Custom types must re-registered session. make persistent, add registration calls .Rprofile, create small package .onLoad() hook.
code: register_qryflow_type("custom", my_custom_handler_func, overwrite = TRUE) ls_qryflow_types()

what: Toy Example: Create query-send Chunk Type
title: Extending qryflow Functionality
text: example shows implement new chunk type ’s similar exec query. create new type, called query-send works like query except calls DBI::dbSendQuery instead DBI::dbGetQuery. First, create handler: Validate hand, ’d like: , register : Check registered properly: test SQL:
code: query_send_handler <- function(con, chunk, ...) {   res <- DBI::dbSendQuery(con, chunk$sql, ...)    results <- DBI::dbFetch(res)    DBI::dbClearResult(res)    results } validate_qryflow_handler(query_send_handler) register_qryflow_type(   "query-send",   handler = query_send_handler,   overwrite = TRUE ) #> [1] TRUE ls_qryflow_types() #> [1] "exec"       "query"      "query-send" # Creates an in-memory sqlite database and populates it with an mtcars table, named "mtcars" con <- example_db_connect(mtcars)  # Create sql <- " -- @query-send: df_mtcars SELECT * FROM mtcars; "  results <- qryflow(con, sql)  head(results) #>    mpg cyl disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb #> 1 21.0   6  160 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4 #> 2 21.0   6  160 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4 #> 3 22.8   4  108  93 3.85 2.320 18.61  1  1    4    1 #> 4 21.4   6  258 110 3.08 3.215 19.44  1  0    3    1 #> 5 18.7   8  360 175 3.15 3.440 17.02  0  0    3    2 #> 6 18.1   6  225 105 2.76 3.460 20.22  1  0    3    1

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: qryflow lets write multi-step SQL workflows plain .sql files run R single function call. Specially formatted tags tell R execute SQL chunk name results. allows : Keep multiple SQL statements file. Control SQL “chunk” executed. Return results named R objects. Pass metadata can used later R workflows short: can define run multi-step SQL workflows one function call, get results back structured R object.

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: main function qryflow, accepts SQL tagged special comments connection DBI-compliant database. Note, SQL can character vector, like example , filepath file contains SQL. default, package supports @exec tags, executed DBI::dbExecute() @query tags, executed DBI::dbGetQuery(). run qryflow(): SQL script split chunks using tag lines like -- @query: df_mtcars. chunk assigned type (e.g., query exec) Chunks executed order, using associated execution type results returned named objects
code: # Connection to In-Memory DB with table populated from mtcars con <- example_db_connect(mtcars)  sql <- " -- @exec: drop_cyl_6 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS cyl_6;  -- @exec: prep_cyl_6 CREATE TABLE cyl_6 AS SELECT * FROM mtcars WHERE cyl = 6;  -- @query: df_cyl_6 SELECT * FROM cyl_6; "  # Pass tagged SQL to `qryflow` results <- qryflow(con, sql, verbose = TRUE) #> Running 3 chunks #> [1/3] drop_cyl_6 [exec] #>       ✓ success  0s #> [2/3] prep_cyl_6 [exec] #>       ✓ success  0s #> [3/3] df_cyl_6 [query] #>       ✓ success  0s #> Done in 0s — 3 success, 0 error, 0 skipped  # Access the results from the chunk named `df_cyl_6` head(results$df_cyl_6) #>    mpg cyl  disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb #> 1 21.0   6 160.0 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4 #> 2 21.0   6 160.0 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4 #> 3 21.4   6 258.0 110 3.08 3.215 19.44  1  0    3    1 #> 4 18.1   6 225.0 105 2.76 3.460 20.22  1  0    3    1 #> 5 19.2   6 167.6 123 3.92 3.440 18.30  1  0    4    4 #> 6 17.8   6 167.6 123 3.92 3.440 18.90  1  0    4    4

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: qryflow, chunk grouped section SQL code, representing single executable unit within larger multi-step SQL workflow, preceded one tag lines (e.g., pattern -- @<tag>: <value>). Tagged lines act markers start new chunk. lines (comments SQL) immediately following contiguous group tagged lines belong chunk another tag line starts next chunk. script tags, entire script treated one single chunk.

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: SQL chunk must tagged type qryflow knows execute . chunk provided tag, qryflow engine use value default_type argument, can provided directly set getOption("qryflow.default_type", "query"). defaults “query”, getting data common use case. Tags use SQL-style comments (--) follow format:
code: -- @<tag>: <value>

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: chunk name (name object returned R) type (execution mode chunk). Users can set explicitly following tags: @type apecifies execution type (-- @type: query) @name assigns name chunk’s result (-- @name: df_users) registered types, users can use shorthand supply name type one line. example, @query @exec aliases setting @type @name one line. Aliased form (preferred): Explicit form (equivalent):
code: -- @query: df_mtcars SELECT * FROM mtcars; -- @type: query -- @name: df_mtcars SELECT * FROM mtcars;

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: parsing, qryflow determines type using following rules: chunk includes explicit -- @type: tag, value used chunk type. @type: tag, qryflow checks tag matches registered type (@query:, @exec:, etc.) . first match found used type. recognized tag found, type defaults value getOption("qryflow.default_type", "query").

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: can include additional tags carry metadata R workflow, follow tagging structure:
code: -- @exec: df_mtcars -- @src: dbo.mtcars -- @topic: cars SELECT * FROM mtcars;

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: on_error argument controls happens single chunk fails: "stop" (default): halts execution immediately raises error. "warn": records error signals warning, continues running remaining chunks. "collect": silently collects errors across chunks raises single combined error end.
code: # on_error = "stop" (default): halts on first failure bad_sql <- " -- @exec: prep_cyl_6 CREATE TABLE cyl_6 AS SELECT * FROM mtcars WHERE cyl = 6;  -- @query: df_missing SELECT * FROM nonexistent_table;  -- @query: df_mtcars SELECT * FROM mtcars; "  qryflow(con, bad_sql, on_error = "stop") #> Error: #> ! Chunk 'prep_cyl_6' failed with message: 'table cyl_6 already exists' # Warn collects errors and signals a warning qryflow(con, bad_sql, on_error = "warn") #> Warning: table cyl_6 already exists #> Warning: no such table: nonexistent_table # on_error = "collect": runs everything, then reports all failures together qryflow(con, bad_sql, verbose = TRUE, on_error = "collect") #> Running 3 chunks #> [1/3] prep_cyl_6 [exec] #>       ✗ error    0s #> [2/3] df_missing [query] #>       ✗ error    0s #> [3/3] df_mtcars [query] #>       ✓ success  0s #> Done in 0s — 0 success, 0 error, 0 skipped #> Error: #> ! 2 chunks failed: #>   - prep_cyl_6: table cyl_6 already exists #>   - df_missing: no such table: nonexistent_table

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: default, qryflow quiet. However, long running queries multiple chunks, may want feedback chunks currently running. can use verbose = TRUE get updates execution.

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: simplify = TRUE, case one chunk, qryflow() return single object (opposed named list results). example: design choice facilitate easy interactive use common use-case. qryflow() might return named list single data.frame depending input, qryflow package exports functions users can prioritize reliability return objects. next section explores functions like qryflow_run() qryflow_results() .
code: sql1 <- " -- @query: df_mtcars SELECT * FROM mtcars; "  sql2 <- " -- @query: df_mtcars SELECT * FROM mtcars;  -- @query: df_mtcars_cyl6 SELECT * FROM mtcars WHERE cyl = 6; "  # Pass tagged SQL to `qryflow` res1 <- qryflow(con, sql1, simplify = TRUE) res2 <- qryflow(con, sql2, simplify = TRUE) res3 <- qryflow(con, sql1, simplify = FALSE)  class(res1) # simplifies the result to the single data.frame() because only one chunk #> [1] "data.frame" class(res2) # returns named list #> [1] "list" class(res3) # returns named list, because simplify = FALSE #> [1] "list"

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: qryflow() covers use cases, users want control consistency may prefer use functions qryflow() leverages: qryflow_run() qrflow_results() qryflow_parse() qryflow_execute

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: qryflow_run() performs parsing execution, returning full qryflow object - including chunk metadata, just query results. element qryflow_chunk: extract query results (equivalent qryflow() returns), use qryflow_results():
code: obj <- qryflow_run(con, sql)  # A qryflow object class(obj) #> [1] "qryflow"  # Chunk names are top-level list names names(obj) #> [1] "drop_cyl_6" "prep_cyl_6" "df_cyl_6"  obj # Print Method #> ── qryflow ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   chunks: 3 | status: success | duration: 0s  #>  #>   drop_cyl_6  [exec ]  ✓ success  0s #>   prep_cyl_6  [exec ]  ✓ success  0s #>   df_cyl_6    [query]  ✓ success  0s class(obj$df_cyl_6) #> [1] "qryflow_chunk"  # Print the chunk obj$df_cyl_6 #> ── qryflow_chunk: df_cyl_6 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   type: query | ✓ success | duration: 0s #>  #>   SELECT * #>   FROM cyl_6; results <- qryflow_results(obj) class(results$df_cyl_6) #> [1] "data.frame" head(results$df_cyl_6) #>    mpg cyl  disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb #> 1 21.0   6 160.0 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4 #> 2 21.0   6 160.0 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4 #> 3 21.4   6 258.0 110 3.08 3.215 19.44  1  0    3    1 #> 4 18.1   6 225.0 105 2.76 3.460 20.22  1  0    3    1 #> 5 19.2   6 167.6 123 3.92 3.440 18.30  1  0    4    4 #> 6 17.8   6 167.6 123 3.92 3.440 18.90  1  0    4    4

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: even control, can parse execute separately: qryflow_chunk contains: $type: execution type (e.g., "query") $name: chunk name $sql: SQL body $tags: additional tags $results: NULL execution; populated
code: # Step 1: Parse the SQL into structured chunks filepath <- example_sql_path() workflow <- qryflow_parse(filepath)  class(workflow) #> [1] "qryflow" length(workflow) #> [1] 4 names(workflow) #> [1] "drop_cyl_6" "prep_cyl_6" "df_mtcars"  "df_cyl_6"  # Inspect a chunk before execution workflow$df_mtcars #> ── qryflow_chunk: df_mtcars ────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   type: query |   pending   #>  #>   SELECT * #>   FROM mtcars; # Step 2: Execute the parsed workflow executed <- qryflow_execute(con, workflow)  class(executed) #> [1] "qryflow" names(executed) #> [1] "drop_cyl_6" "prep_cyl_6" "df_mtcars"  "df_cyl_6" executed #> ── qryflow ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  #>   chunks: 4 | status: success | duration: 0s  #>  #>   drop_cyl_6  [exec ]  ✓ success  0s #>   prep_cyl_6  [exec ]  ✓ success  0s #>   df_mtcars   [query]  ✓ success  0s #>   df_cyl_6    [query]  ✓ success  0s

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: worfklow object (qryflow) chunk objects (qryflow_chunk) store metadata execution. can access information qryflow_meta() function:

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: guide registering custom chunk types extending qryflow’s behaviour, see vignette("extend-qryflow", package = "qryflow").

title: Getting Started with qryflow
text: Example 1 - Script tags Result - entire script one chunk containing lines. ? Without tags, qryflow treats whole script single step. Example 2 - Script one tag start Result - One chunk starting tag, containing rest script. tag line 1, chunk starts continues end. Example 3 - Script one tag middle Result - Two chunks: Chunk 1: lines tag (untagged SQL). Chunk 2: tag line end. preserves pre-tag SQL separate chunk. Example 4 - Script multiple tags Result - Four chunks, starting respective tag line. chunk parsed executed independently sequence.
code: CREATE TABLE cyl_6 AS SELECT * FROM mtcars WHERE cyl = 6; -- @query: get_6cyl SELECT * FROM mtcars WHERE cyl = 6; SELECT * FROM mtcars WHERE cyl = 6;  -- @query: df_mtcars SELECT * FROM mtcars; -- @exec: drop_cyl_6 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS cyl_6;  -- @exec: prep_cyl_6 CREATE TABLE cyl_6 AS SELECT * FROM mtcars WHERE cyl = 6;  -- @query: df_mtcars SELECT * FROM mtcars;  -- @query: df_cyl_6 SELECT * FROM cyl_6

title: Execute Multi-Step SQL Workflows
text: qryflow lets write multi-step SQL workflows plain .sql files run R single function call. Specially formatted tags tell R execute SQL chunk name results. allows : Keep multiple SQL statements file. Control SQL “chunk” executed. Return results named R objects.

title: Execute Multi-Step SQL Workflows
text: can install released version qryflow CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("qryflow") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("christian-million/qryflow")

title: Execute Multi-Step SQL Workflows
text: Basic Usage: path file containing SQL can also passed:
code: library(qryflow)  # Connection to In-Memory DB with table populated from mtcars con <- example_db_connect(mtcars)  sql <- " -- @exec: drop_cyl_6 DROP TABLE IF EXISTS cyl_6;  -- @exec: prep_cyl_6 CREATE TABLE cyl_6 AS SELECT * FROM mtcars WHERE cyl = 6;  -- @query: df_mtcars SELECT * FROM mtcars;  -- @query: df_cyl_6 SELECT * FROM cyl_6; "  # Pass tagged SQL to `qryflow` results <- qryflow(con, sql, verbose = TRUE) #> Running 4 chunks #> [1/4] drop_cyl_6 [exec] #>       ✓ success  0s #> [2/4] prep_cyl_6 [exec] #>       ✓ success  0s #> [3/4] df_mtcars [query] #>       ✓ success  0s #> [4/4] df_cyl_6 [query] #>       ✓ success  0s #> Done in 0.01s — 4 success, 0 error, 0 skipped  # Access the results from the chunk named `df_cyl_6` head(results$df_cyl_6) #>    mpg cyl  disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb #> 1 21.0   6 160.0 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4 #> 2 21.0   6 160.0 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4 #> 3 21.4   6 258.0 110 3.08 3.215 19.44  1  0    3    1 #> 4 18.1   6 225.0 105 2.76 3.460 20.22  1  0    3    1 #> 5 19.2   6 167.6 123 3.92 3.440 18.30  1  0    4    4 #> 6 17.8   6 167.6 123 3.92 3.440 18.90  1  0    4    4 filepath <- example_sql_path('mtcars.sql')  # Pass tagged SQL to `qryflow` results <- qryflow(con, filepath, verbose = TRUE) #> Running 4 chunks #> [1/4] drop_cyl_6 [exec] #>       ✓ success  0s #> [2/4] prep_cyl_6 [exec] #>       ✓ success  0s #> [3/4] df_mtcars [query] #>       ✓ success  0s #> [4/4] df_cyl_6 [query] #>       ✓ success  0s #> Done in 0.01s — 4 success, 0 error, 0 skipped  # Access the results from the chunk named `df_cyl_6` head(results$df_cyl_6) #>    mpg cyl  disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb #> 1 21.0   6 160.0 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4 #> 2 21.0   6 160.0 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4 #> 3 21.4   6 258.0 110 3.08 3.215 19.44  1  0    3    1 #> 4 18.1   6 225.0 105 2.76 3.460 20.22  1  0    3    1 #> 5 19.2   6 167.6 123 3.92 3.440 18.30  1  0    4    4 #> 6 17.8   6 167.6 123 3.92 3.440 18.90  1  0    4    4

title: Execute Multi-Step SQL Workflows
text: Consider following vignettes depth understanding: Getting Started: Outlines available features, use qryflow, provides operational understanding works (vignette("getting-started", package = "qryflow")). Extend qryflow: guide understanding implement custom tags, override built-tags, using custom chunk handlers (vignette("extend-qryflow", package = "qryflow")).

title: Execute Multi-Step SQL Workflows
text: functionality made available qryflow exists packages. However, scope implementation qryflow makes distinct enough justify unique package. recommend reviewing packages see works best needs. feel list incomplete, please submit issue: sqlhelper provides comprehensive tools executing parameterized SQL scripts, managing database connections configurations, supporting spatial data types, statement-level control within SQL files. SQLove ‘lightweight R package handling complex SQL scripts including temp tables, multiple queries, etc.’

what: Extract tagged metadata from a SQL chunk — extract_all_tags
title: Extract tagged metadata from a SQL chunk — extract_all_tags
text: extract_all_tags() scans SQL specially formatted comment tags (e.g., -- @tag: value) returns named list. exported intent useful users extending qryflow. typically used single SQL chunk, one parsed .sql file.

title: Extract tagged metadata from a SQL chunk — extract_all_tags
code: extract_all_tags(text)  subset_tags(tags, keep, negate = FALSE)

title: Extract tagged metadata from a SQL chunk — extract_all_tags
text: text character vector SQL lines file path SQL script. tags named list tags, typically extract_all_tags(). Used subset_tags(). keep character vector tag names keep exclude subset_tags(). negate Logical; TRUE, subset_tags() returns tags except listed keep.

title: Extract tagged metadata from a SQL chunk — extract_all_tags
text: extract_all_tags(): named list tags found SQL chunk. subset_tags(): filtered named list tags NULL none remain.

title: Extract tagged metadata from a SQL chunk — extract_all_tags
code: filepath <- example_sql_path('mtcars.sql') parsed <- qryflow_parse(filepath)  chunk <- parsed[[1]] tags <- extract_all_tags(chunk$sql) subset_tags(tags, keep = c("query")) #> list()

title: Detect the presence of a properly structured tagline — is_tag_line

code: a <- "-- @query: df_mtcars" b <- "-- @exec: prep_tbl" c <- "-- @type: query"  lines <- c(a, b, c)  is_tag_line(lines) #> [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE

what: Run a multi-step SQL workflow and return query results — qryflow
title: Run a multi-step SQL workflow and return query results — qryflow
text: qryflow() high level convenience function. executes SQL workflow defined tagged .sql script character string returns query results R objects. SQL script can contain multiple-steps (chunks), tagged @query @exec. Query results captured returned named list, names correspond @query tags.

title: Run a multi-step SQL workflow and return query results — qryflow
code: qryflow(   con,   sql,   ...,   on_error = c("stop", "warn", "collect"),   verbose = getOption("qryflow.verbose", FALSE),   simplify = TRUE,   default_type = getOption("qryflow.default_type", "query") )

title: Run a multi-step SQL workflow and return query results — qryflow
text: con database connection DBI::dbConnect() sql file path .sql workflow character string containing SQL code. ... Additional arguments passed qryflow_run() qryflow_results(). on_error Controls behaviour chunk fails execution. One "stop" (default), "warn", "collect". "stop" halts execution immediately returns partially executed workflow. "warn" records error chunk's meta, signaling immediately. "collect" gathers errors across chunks reports end. verbose Logical. TRUE, emits message chunk identifying name type, prints summary completion reporting total chunks run, successes, errors, elapsed time. Defaults FALSE. global default can set options(qryflow.verbose = TRUE). simplify Logical; TRUE (default), list length 1 simplified single result object. default_type default chunk type (defaults "query"). global default can set options(qryflow.default_type = 'query').

title: Run a multi-step SQL workflow and return query results — qryflow
text: (Invisibly) named list query results, single result simplify = TRUE one chunk exists.

title: Run a multi-step SQL workflow and return query results — qryflow
text: wrapper around combination qryflow_run(), always provides list results metadata, qryflow_results(), filters output qryflow_run() include results SQL.

title: Run a multi-step SQL workflow and return query results — qryflow
code: con <- example_db_connect(mtcars) filepath <- example_sql_path("mtcars.sql")  results <- qryflow(con, filepath)  head(results$df_mtcars) #>    mpg cyl disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb #> 1 21.0   6  160 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4 #> 2 21.0   6  160 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4 #> 3 22.8   4  108  93 3.85 2.320 18.61  1  1    4    1 #> 4 21.4   6  258 110 3.08 3.215 19.44  1  0    3    1 #> 5 18.7   8  360 175 3.15 3.440 17.02  0  0    3    2 #> 6 18.1   6  225 105 2.76 3.460 20.22  1  0    3    1  DBI::dbDisconnect(con)

what: Execute a parsed qryflow SQL workflow — qryflow_execute
title: Execute a parsed qryflow SQL workflow — qryflow_execute
text: qryflow_execute() takes qryflow object (returned qryflow_parse()), executes chunk (e.g., @query, @exec), collects results timing metadata. function used internally qryflow_run(), can called directly concert qryflow_parse() want manually control parsing execution.

title: Execute a parsed qryflow SQL workflow — qryflow_execute
code: qryflow_execute(   con,   x,   ...,   on_error = c("stop", "warn", "collect"),   verbose = getOption("qryflow.verbose", FALSE) )

title: Execute a parsed qryflow SQL workflow — qryflow_execute
text: con database connection DBI::dbConnect() x qryflow object, typically created qryflow_parse() ... Reserved future use on_error Controls behaviour chunk fails execution. One "stop" (default), "warn", "collect". "stop" halts execution immediately returns partially executed workflow. "warn" records error chunk's meta, signaling immediately. "collect" gathers errors across chunks reports end. verbose Logical. TRUE, emits message chunk identifying name type, prints summary completion reporting total chunks run, successes, errors, elapsed time. Defaults FALSE. global default can set options(qryflow.verbose = TRUE).

title: Execute a parsed qryflow SQL workflow — qryflow_execute
text: object class qryflow, containing executed chunks results meta attribute includes timing source information.

title: Execute a parsed qryflow SQL workflow — qryflow_execute
code: con <- example_db_connect(mtcars)  filepath <- example_sql_path("mtcars.sql")  parsed <- qryflow_parse(filepath)  executed <- qryflow_execute(con, parsed)  DBI::dbDisconnect(con)

what: Parse and execute a tagged SQL workflow — qryflow_run
title: Parse and execute a tagged SQL workflow — qryflow_run
text: qryflow_run() reads SQL workflow file path character string, parses tagged statements, executes statements database connection. function might preferable want qryflow execution consistently return qryflow object. Whereas qryflow() function may return list objects, depending arguments, qryflow_run() always returns qryflow object. Results can extracted using qryflow_results().

title: Parse and execute a tagged SQL workflow — qryflow_run
code: qryflow_run(   con,   sql,   ...,   on_error = c("stop", "warn", "collect"),   verbose = getOption("qryflow.verbose", FALSE),   default_type = getOption("qryflow.default_type", "query") )

title: Parse and execute a tagged SQL workflow — qryflow_run
text: con database connection DBI::dbConnect() sql character string representing either path .sql file raw SQL content. ... Additional arguments passed qryflow_execute(). on_error Controls behaviour chunk fails execution. One "stop" (default), "warn", "collect". "stop" halts execution immediately returns partially executed workflow. "warn" records error chunk's meta, signaling immediately. "collect" gathers errors across chunks reports end. verbose Logical. TRUE, emits message chunk identifying name type, prints summary completion reporting total chunks run, successes, errors, elapsed time. Defaults FALSE. global default can set options(qryflow.verbose = TRUE). default_type default chunk type (defaults "query"). global default can set options(qryflow.default_type = TRUE).

title: Parse and execute a tagged SQL workflow — qryflow_run
text: (Invisibly) list representing evaluated workflow, containing query results, execution metadata, , depending contents SQL script.

title: Parse and execute a tagged SQL workflow — qryflow_run

title: Standardizes lines read from string, character vector, or file — read_sql_lines

title: Standardizes lines read from string, character vector, or file — read_sql_lines

title: Standardizes lines read from string, character vector, or file — read_sql_lines
text: qryflow_sql object (inherits character) length equal number lines read

title: Standardizes lines read from string, character vector, or file — read_sql_lines
code: # From a file ##### path <- example_sql_path() read_sql_lines(path) #> <qryflow_sql> #> -- @exec: drop_cyl_6 #> DROP TABLE IF EXISTS cyl_6; #>  #> -- @exec: prep_cyl_6 #> CREATE TABLE cyl_6 AS #> SELECT * #> FROM mtcars #> WHERE cyl = 6; #>  #> -- @query: df_mtcars #> SELECT * #> FROM mtcars; #>  #> -- @query: df_cyl_6 #> SELECT * #> FROM cyl_6;  # From a single string ##### sql <- "SELECT * FROM mtcars;" read_sql_lines(sql) #> <qryflow_sql> #> SELECT * #> FROM mtcars;  # From a character ##### lines <- c("SELECT *", "FROM mtcars;") read_sql_lines(lines) #> <qryflow_sql> #> SELECT * #> FROM mtcars;

title: qryflow (development version)
text: source argument removed qryflow_execute(). Source information now stored automatically chunk metadata parsing. qryflow_default_type() removed standalone exported function. Default type now controlled directly via default_type argument qryflow(), qryflow_run(), qryflow_parse(), global option options(qryflow.default_type = "query"). Note: option key changed qryflow.default.type qryflow.default_type.

title: qryflow (development version)
text: on_error argument added qryflow(), qryflow_run(), qryflow_execute(). Controls behavior chunk fails execution. Accepts "stop" (default, halts immediately), "warn" (signals warning continues), "collect" (runs chunks reports errors together end). (#11 #12 verbose argument added qryflow(), qryflow_run(), qryflow_execute(). TRUE, emits progress message chunk summary upon completion showing total runtime, successes, errors, skipped chunks. Defaults FALSE. Can enabled globally options(qryflow.verbose = TRUE). ([#8]) default_type argument added qryflow(), qryflow_run(), qryflow_parse(). Determines chunk type assigned untagged chunks. Defaults "query". Can set globally options(qryflow.default_type = "query"). qryflow_meta() now exported function accessing execution metadata (status, duration, start/end times, error messages) qryflow workflow objects individual qryflow_chunk objects. validate_con_arg() now called internally qryflow_execute() provide clear, actionable error messages connection argument missing, invalid, disconnected. Workflow-level execution status now tracked stored metadata. completed workflow assigned status "success" chunks succeeded, "partial" chunk encountered error.

title: qryflow (development version)
text: Combined qryflow_workflow qryflow_results single object. ([#14]) Added cli.R verbose helpers (report_workflow_start(), report_chunk_start(), report_chunk_end(), report_workflow_end()). Added -error.R consolidating error dispatch logic (validate_on_error(), dispatch_on_error(), dispatch_collected_errors()) supporting new on_error argument. Metadata handling refactored meta.R, centralizing time tracking duration formatting used across workflow chunk objects. Updated documentation, vignettes, README reflect new arguments removed functions.

title: qryflow 0.3.0
text: type-specific parser system removed (#13) . register_qryflow_parser(), ls_qryflow_parsers() validate_qryflow_parser(), qryflow_parser_exists() longer available. Custom chunk behaviour now implemented entirely handlers. See register_qryflow_type() details. ls_qryflow_handlers() register_qryflow_handler() removed. Use ls_qryflow_types() register_qryflow_type() instead.

title: qryflow 0.3.0
text: Parsing now handled single unified parser produces consistent qryflow_chunk structure regardless chunk type. replaces previous system chunk type define parsing logic. User provided duplicate names now generates error via refactored approach fix_chunk_names() (Now, resolve_chunk_names()) Updated documentation, README, vignettes accomodate unified parsing.

title: qryflow 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-05 Breaking change: qryflow(), qryflow_run(), qryflow_execute() internal functions now accept con argument first, sql/workflow arguments. makes API consistent DBI DB packages, improves ergonomics, enables method dispatch connection classes. (#5) Minor documentation updates (#2) Update License Year (#6)

title: qryflow 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-07-18 Initial CRAN submission.
